topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The spreadsheet glowed on my monitor, a stark, unforgiving landscape of numbers. I traced the line item for quarterly bonuses, my fingertip hovering over the figures until it settled on the disparity. Sarah’s salary, juxtaposed with mine, felt like a physical weight pressing down on my chest. I didn't look up when Mark approached, his usual bright energy momentarily subdued by the silence between us. "Hey, [My Name]," he said, offering a small, tentative smile. I nodded, a slow, deliberate motion. The air in the cubicle seemed thick, heavy with unspoken comparisons. I gathered my papers, the sound of the shuffling paper the loudest noise in the office. I filed the report, the act mechanical, devoid of any real engagement. Tomorrow would be the same.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen until the numbers swam, blurring into meaningless streaks of green and red. It was an undeniable fact, this gap in compensation between me and Alex. A strange, flat calm settled over me, like the surface of still water. I took a slow sip of lukewarm coffee, the bitterness a familiar anchor. When Alex walked past my desk, his laughter echoing down the hall, I simply watched him go. There was no surge of anger, no sudden urge to confront him. Just a quiet acknowledgment of the reality. I returned to my keyboard, typing the next task, my movements economical and practiced. The realization had settled in my gut, a dull ache that required no dramatic outburst.

[story 3]

The realization hit me during the mandatory team meeting. When the VP mentioned the departmental budget allocations, the numbers flashed on the screen, and the difference between my pay grade and the one assigned to Chloe became painfully clear. I kept my expression neutral, focusing intently on the speaker, absorbing the words as if they held profound meaning. Inside, a slow deflation occurred. It wasn't a shock; it was more like watching a tide slowly recede. I made a note to myself, a silent cataloging of the information, and then I simply nodded along when it was my turn to speak. The weight of the situation simply became another fixture in the landscape of my daily existence.
